I'm just an opener. There is absolutely no way under the sun that if you can be a proficient opener That you can't be a proficient closer It's just bullshit now That being said there are many companies where you have to earn your dues Gotta pay your dues right those you have to actually work and be an opener because you're feeding the machine You're feeding a system whereby you're getting started you're being trained you're being mentored right and you know You're providing qualified leads through someone who's already been through that process So you're providing a service for the company. But You know, it's almost like that. It's almost like the it's the throwaway side to the you know, because of course we want the buyers, right? So what really interested is the buyers The people who Fall into one of two categories the first category we call our buyers In heat And a buyer in heat Is your absolute best buyer, right? That's someone who needs your product They want your product They value your product and they're feeling pain Right now they're feeling some discomfort about or a lack from not having That need being there's a need they need to be filled with an itch They got a scratch something that's making their life feel out of control in some way That's a buyer in heat So they need your product They want your product and in their own mind They've already made a decision that yes, I gotta do this now So the operative word here what really separates us. This is best buyer Is the word now They've made a decision That I gotta fill this need now. It's a pressing need. It's not just the need. It's an urgent need Right now that being said, okay, doesn't mean they're gonna buy your product They haven't maybe made a decision to buy your particular brand or from you But they has said I gotta fill this need just because they are feeling pain Doesn't mean the other elements of the straight line don't have to line up, right? They still have to trust you Trust the company that stands behind the product, right the pain number five is that was pain But they trust you trust the company also think that your product is gonna be the best one the best fit so to speak, right? But the key here the key is that these buyers, right? They have made a decision to buy now near-term buyers, right as opposed To the other set of buyers here, right which are called your buyers In power So you have buyers in heat Which are your absolute best buyers? They are feeling pain. They're out of control. They got to get back in control and your product so to speak Serves as the bomb for that You get it? They have a pain. You're the bomb Simple right buyers in power right or every bit as good As buyers in heat the only difference is they haven't Decided on this one word Now They have not said I have to buy now. They'll say yeah, like I'm gonna buy I need this product I want this product And it'll make my life better But I can wait around I'm in position of power the buyer in heat doesn't feel like he's in power He's sort of out of control. And then that leaves the other half of the pipeline To be your what's called your looky lose Or tire tickers Looky lose a tire ticker and these are the people Who I consider To be the most dangerous people of all to your success in the sales field Your tire tickers your buyers your your looky lose what these people do And tire kickers comes from the auto industry, right? And lookaloo's is from retail. They just come in there. You know, they look at everything. They want to touch everything, right? They don't buy The reason they're so destructive to you Is that they disguise themselves? They disguise themselves As buyers in power Not buyers in heat Just I want you to really understand what's going on You'll be able to start making your decisions on the fly of who you should really take down the straight line And who you shouldn't and that gets back to that first thing I said today is about really time management So much of succeeding at sales at the highest level is learned to be an excellent time manager Intradiate how to move and groove and not get bogged down in the bullshit That's so many salesmen do that slow down number of tiles they make and also just take them off course by Speaking to unqualified people who never buy Right. We have to to really make this your year You have to master so I gotta dig deeper into the psychology of what's really going on. So watch Someone who's a buyer in heat They're easy to recognize They are so excited. They say things. I'll tell you they'll tell you about their pain as soon as you start asking questions I'll go. Oh my god. Let me tell you my tale. Oh, whoa. It's They're Not their heart, but they wear their pain on their sleeves As the phrase goes they're easy to spot, right? The buyers in power Are easy to spot too because they're very attentive They're typically informed. They need your product. They want your product. They've probably done some research about it, right? These people here though Are the tricksters those like, you know, Loki from Avengers, right? These are the people that disguise themselves as buyers in power So to fully understand how to really weed them out of your pipeline, you have to know What's really behind them? Why would people do that? Why would someone want to waste your time? And really what is the the main thing that makes a look at a little retire kicker attire kicker? So I'm gonna reveal that now. I mean, I've said it in the past here, but I want to get laser guard here So let's dig into this number one The biggest thing the number one thing that separates a lookie-loo From a buyer in power is what? They guess anyone? Scratch money 90% of the time seriously 90% of the time They just can't afford it And they want to experience the thrill of touching something or looking at or getting it and feeling a big shot from it in a car As if they can afford it. Maybe they show it off for something important with them Or they just want to you experience it. This way take the test drive or whatever it might be try on the clothes the driving force The driving force behind lookie-loos is money Don't kid yourself. That's what it's about So what I want you to start doing right now is paying a lot closer attention To the financial aspects of your qualification If you find yourself With a lot of people who you seem like they were seem really interested But then just for this is where they earned that to be you'll tell because your clothes are gonna be much lower than it should be You'll have your life I just really really interested you run the whole straight line on them, right and just time it again They're not buying from you That is because of money That's the big one. So you have to start sifting these people out Upfront see what happens is let me explain The biggest problem of a lookie-loo is that it's besides the time that you waste with them Is that you start thinking that there's something wrong with yourself or your pitch Or your methods you say god, I don't get it like you know I spoke to I pitched 10 people today or I pitched 20 people and only one person bought And it was like seven of them in there that were just like they were They were so interested they seemed so great But like I just couldn't get them over the line even I dropped the amount to buy and you load their agate didn't work My Again, I can't dig into your business without looking at it first hand But my guess is gonna be here is that most of the time That low closing rate was a function Of you not spending enough time weeding out the non buyers from the buyers And you ended up making full blown presentation to people who can't fucking buy your product if they're life dependent Because they just can't afford it You get it. They can't afford it And because of that You start developing all these self-bounds and limiting beliefs You get it now Now we go back to the beginning Why so okay knowing that and by the way, it's not even At this point it can't be like a novel thought like Because I've said this before I've told you how toxic these people are right So whether you've been through the formal straight line trading out even in these intensives and daily motivations, right? When the single biggest way to shoot yourself in the foot Is by taking all the look you lose down your down the straight line. You'll kill yourself doing that. Why you know why? How can I make that many calls? It's the mistaken belief that you have about how many times you can dial the phone Or how many doors you can knock on or simply what's the commitment you're making to your advertising budget to your lead generation program guys if you don't have enough leads to call Inevitably You will let the look you lose in You will you it's too tempting and they're too devilishly clever at convincing you that they're buyers in power Even for myself who's the world's foremost expert in studies this stuff, right? If I am in a position where I'm short on leads I have to actively beg myself not to try to turn look you lose into fucking buyers It's just human nature Now I stop myself from doing it I I'm able to because I'm just so well trained at this point. You don't have 50 years of this stuff behind you Okay, and no the the as soon as your lead source dries up the Pull the path of the the it's so it's just strong a magnetic attraction to just take Shit and try to turn it into gold Can't do it In the formal training Of the straight line. I always say step one We are not alchemists We don't turn lead into gold. We don't turn one thing into another We're salespeople. We're sifters. We sift through people like the proverbial Tin pan or with the scraggly beard and his tin pan and he sits by a stream of fast running water Why fast running because the faster runs the more water the more molecules that go through his pan He sifts and he sifts and he sifts and he knows that if he sits there long enough And waits till enough gallons come through eventually A nugget of gold will drop in his pan But what he's not expecting to happen Because it's physically impossible. It can't happen Is that if you wait till the fucking heavens explode the water's not going to turn into gold They it's not mutable. We don't what is water gold is gold Buyers are buyers non buyers are not unnone buys. They don't change you What a salesman does watch and here's the distinction And I hope I hope I'm beating a dead horse Because this dead horse needs to get beaten because it's life or death to a salesperson What you do as a salesperson is you turn buyers In power Into what? Buyers in heat And then you close them That's what you're doing as you take someone down the straight line In other words in the pipeline of a hundred people you probably have maybe 10 to 20 tops buyers in heat Really? Let's call this let's let's make it let's call 15 15 buyers in heat They need your product. They want your product and they need it now. They're in pain They made decision. They're in pain right that they're the best buyers of all, but they're few in numbers Far bigger Are your buyers so this is your buyers in heat? Okay. Now. Here's the trick When you qualify right you're obviously sifting You're sifting through that let's use that 100 people you're sifting through the 100 people But you're also also In the business of amplifying What pain? Amplifying what pain? In other words the difference between a buyer in power And a buyer in heat is that the buyer in power is not feeling pain or out of control discomfort They're probably in denial They've been in pain so long just want to feel anymore, right? But the point is is that you're not just sifting You're not just sifting you're also as you the reason you ask Qualifying questions in the way you do and using certain tonalities Okay, and you ask pain literally so how long has been a problem for You'll act listen to the reason you ask certain questions Is because they're specifically designed to amplify pain You get it So it's not enough All right, how long way to go Sivir? How long? You're about 50 minutes Okay, so I want to keep going so So look it's not enough If you just if you think that going through A stack of leads, you know of raw leads to get your to you know to your prospecting Is just to sift you're that you're missing the story here Okay, remember To close the the biggest source of buyers It at the end of the end of the day, right? Most of the people who bought from you. Whoa There were buyers in power not buyers need there's not as many of them. So The majority of your closed sales will come from buyers in power Not buyers in heat and the journey From a buyer in power to a closed sale Includes a stopping off point as a buyer in heat They don't go from power To closed It doesn't happen That's like dry ice Dry ice is this amazing thing It engages in a process called sublimation Sublimation is when you skip something So what when you go from a solid phase To a gash gashes phase right solid to gas What's missing water? Usually you melt something you've ice you melt it becomes liquid and then the liquid evaporates it becomes gas right? That's the normal process Sublimation is like dry ice when it goes from frozen carbon dioxide goes straight from a solid to a gas you skip a step Guess what that process of sublimation It does not exist in sales You don't have sublimation in other words you have to go buyer in power Gets transformed to a buyer in heat And then he's convinced to buy now That's what's happening and the process the main part of that Occurs while you're prospecting Not while you're closing and you're always closing because but not in your looping. That's later on Looping is designed to build certainty And then in your last step in the process you then reintroduce pain But the point of prospecting is yes, of course to sift. Yeah, you're sifting right? but just as importantly you're beginning or really making a major shot at taking a buyer in power And turning him into a buyer in heat That's the key And at the same time you're doing that now we get again. We were always doing many things at once, right? Also, you're accomplishing step three Which is you're building massive rapport And the reason that's so important Is because without that rapport If you try to take someone from being a buyer in power to a buyer, they'll say fuck you Who the fuck you know, I hate you stop you You don't create pain in someone Without being in rapport and remain friends with them. There's someone okay. Let's see On some level I hate using the word pain But it's a great word to use so I use it, but I don't need it In like this disempowering Evil sense. Okay, just let's be clear here. So it's not like pain. You're not trying to cause someone pain It's not about that and when I talk about taking a person from you know, who's not feeling pain to making them feel pain I'm not even saying that But I'm it's a shortcut to saying Taking someone who's in denial of their pain And making them realize that they're actually not living the life they want They're not experiencing the full joy the full boat the full the full monty of the whole thing because it's human nature That as we are feeling discomfort For too long We become comfortably numb to it It's a self-protective mechanism It is So what happens is someone That has a lack a certain lack in an aspect of their life and you think they should be feeling some Discomfort they should be feeling out of control. They should be worried about that I'm doing a training for an insurance company life insurance, right? And I've done many life insurance companies and you find all the time a woman and you find the woman and She should be worried fucking to death That if she dies The kid or the husband dies they have their fucked they fucked they have no money set aside for the kids There's no nothing, you know, there's no plan in place and a little bit of life insurance that would cost almost nothing Could put them in the most amazing spot of feeling good about it That if they really were thinking about it logically they'd be fucking panic-stricken And one day they wake up and they are sometimes just too late Sometimes they get lucky and they get insurance for it The point is is that when I talk about Pain waking up there pain me. It's it's an ethical thing To have someone who's walking a ticking time bomb walker Is it ethical for some of the guys walking out with like some sort of medical disease, right disease? And he is in denial Just want to think about it and then the doctor ratchets up in his pain Let him know that he could you know, he's the ticking time bomb. Of course, that's ethical You're not using pain to hurt people So I don't want to say pain based cell and don't mean about hurting people manipulating people. I'm talking about the weird human um, you know Evolutionary protective mechanism we have whereby we go into fucking denial like that we become Totally numb to our own pain so it can go on and still be okay On some level it's like, you know goes back to the enemy of great is what? good Because when you're feeling good There's no pain to change But you miss all the beauty the the greatness of life when you're great when you're extraordinary Same thing goes in sales So I want the key here. It's a flip. It's a flip of perspective Okay, is that and this is when we start to get to the straight line to really higher levels here of what is really going on when you Prospect yes, of course you're sifting out people. You're separating out your non buyers From your buyers and you're building rapport. So they like you because people don't buy from the little yeah, blah, blah But that's the basic straight line But when we start to dig into the psychology of the straight line of why it works Which allows you to use it so much more effectively It's because Of the journey that every buyer goes through before they say yes and that journey Starts from going from a buyer unless they're already in heat and they're obvious, but they're a buyer in power and then you Make them aware That they actually need to be looking at this now And they become a buyer in heat And then you raise up their levels of certainty so high Above their action threshold and bam People buy which is why the last step of course is what we add on pain again So what's happening to be clear here is that in when we prospect So in this journey of taking a buyer in power And turning into a buyer in heat in that journey, right? We we essentially raise up their pain threshold, but we don't resolve the pain not yet So it's not like you get to the bottom and say yeah, I really worry. Oh, don't worry. I got us Hold off. That's not what experts do Expert, huh? I see it. Hmm. We amplify pain. Uh-huh. Oh, how long is that and do you see that in five years? That could be really tricky Huh? Yeah Use the right tonality But now what you've done is you've created a real you've taken a buyer in power And turned them into a buyer in heat and sometimes in admittedly so it doesn't all happen While you're qualifying In fact more often than not you can't have them make the full journey From a buyer in power to a buyer heat. But then again, we go back and we do it again at the end Because maybe you couldn't complete the journey And while they're in power While they're a buyer in power You could have them at a 10 9 9 They still might not buy They have a lot of high accessional they have a very large especially have a lot of access right? But the point is is that last step of the syntax Before you go into, you know after sales, right? Is we add on pain Is because that Is where you're gonna complete you get a single deal you're gonna complete the journey From them going through a buyer in power To a buyer in heat and then you're gonna end by saying why don't we do this and you go into a closing pattern and you close them That's the secret And you know why it's interesting because like when I started tonight I knew I was going to talk about prospecting But I wanted to you know, it's like I wanted to do it in a different way And it wasn't quite I had this message I wanted to get outside about look you lose and stuff and I'm so glad that I hit on this tonight Because I never really talked about the straight line this way before it's like my own almost my own new perspective on it You know, it's I thought this before but I verbalize it in the way that I think is very helpful Connecting the journey of a buyer in other words.
